rationale: The subject home at 835 S Cobble Creek, Nixa, MO features a complex multi-plane hip and gable roof clad in what appears to be asphalt shingles in a uniform medium gray/taupe tone. Across all visible roof slopes — upper north-facing planes, lower south-facing planes, and the multiple hip returns — shingle color and texture are consistent with no visible gaps, dark patches, or exposed decking. No tarps, mismatched repair patches, or biological growth (moss/algae) are present. Ridge lines appear geometrically straight with no visible sag or distortion. A small rooftop protrusion near the upper center is consistent with a standard plumbing vent and is not a condition concern. No debris or staining patterns were identified on any slope. Tree canopy partially borders the roof perimeter but does not obstruct assessment of the primary roof surfaces. Based on what is clearly visible at this resolution, no condition findings meet the confidence threshold for reporting.
